title: main.c: ret variable uninitialized on some code paths
description:
In main.c, the variable 'ret' declared at line 62 may be used uninitialized when CONFIG_USB_DEVICE_STACK is not defined and HAS_LED is not defined.

Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/src/main.c:62

Code path:
- Line 62: int ret; (declared, uninitialized)
- Line 66-75: HAS_LED block (only runs if LED exists) - sets ret
- Line 77-86: USB block (only runs if CONFIG_USB_DEVICE_STACK) - sets ret
- Line 93+: CONFIG_LICHEN_LORA_L2 block - uses ret (line 105, 115, etc.)

What goes wrong: If neither HAS_LED nor CONFIG_USB_DEVICE_STACK is defined, but CONFIG_LICHEN_LORA_L2 is defined, ret is used uninitialized on line 105. In practice this is benign because lichen_lora_l2_init() returns a fresh value, but the pattern violates C best practices and triggers compiler warnings with -Wuninitialized.

Fix: Initialize ret to 0 at declaration.
